steps.title: Identify the constant pattern
steps.content:
The two censuses reported similar counts of vertebrate, plant, and algal species—yet total species nearly doubled.

Since new species descriptions only “partly” explain this gap and microorganisms have poorly understood morphology with “consequential” researcher decisions, the discrepancy must reflect different classification decisions about the same organisms.

steps.title: Match the classification decision
steps.content:
B specifies the classification difference: Bianchi and Morri treated microorganism variations as within-species differences, while Coll treated them as separate species.

A contradicts “only partly” by claiming “largely,” C indirectly addresses sensitivity without naming the classification distinction, and D reverses the relationship.

This matches choice B—B is correct.
